description: A broad stretch of land with little change in elevation is a 'wemo'. Vegetation and land use vary; the word concerns the landform. 'shupi' (plateau) is similarly level ground at a marked elevation above its surroundings.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for the 'w' glide, then open into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', let the voice pass through the nose, and finish with middle-height rounded 'o'.

semantic_domains.nature: a broad landform with little local relief
semantic_domains.spatial: open level terrain across which routes and views extend
